messages.content:
When should most individuals begin to plan for retirement?

 A. 
when they are ten years from retirement and are making the highest incomes of their careers
 B. 
when they are married and have two incomes to draw retirement savings from
 C. 
when they are young and can begin a habit of setting aside funds to a retirement plan
 D. 
when they are five years from retirement and can begin drawing Social Security payments
C. when they are young and can begin a habit of setting aside funds to a retirement plan.

Which of the following statements is true regarding foreclosure?

 A. 
It increases your credit score.
 B. 
It is a legal proceeding.
 C. 
It allows the bank to reclaim your vehicle.
 D. 
It can happen if you are making regular monthly payments.
B. It is a legal proceeding.

Jasmine has excellent credit, and she is looking to purchase a new car that costs $25,000. She has multiple interest rate offers on a car loan. Which offer would save Jasmine the most amount of money?

 A. 
Bank C is offering a 2.7% interest rate for this loan.
 B. 
Bank A is offering a 2% interest rate for this loan.
 C. 
Dealer is offering a 0% interest rate for a new car, plus $1,000 off the price.
 D. 
Bank B is offering a 2.5% interest rate for this loan.
C. Dealer is offering a 0% interest rate for a new car, plus $1,000 off the price.

Which three the following payments does a consumer usually make upon signing a leasing agreement for a house?

 security deposit
 processing fee
 property tax
 mortgage fee
 application fee
Security deposit, processing fee, application fee.

When is an ideal time for someone to purchase a home?

 A. 
when the housing market has grown rapidly and homes are valued at much higher prices
 B. 
when mortgage rates are high
 C. 
when the housing market is depressed and homes may be valued at lower prices
 D. 
when the home buyer is eligible for tax exemption
C. when the housing market is depressed and homes may be valued at lower prices.

Kim's taxable income is about $72,000 a year after accounting for deductions. Based on the following tax table and the use of tax brackets in the United States, which sentence about the taxes payable is true?

Taxable Income	Tax Rate
$0 to $9,875	10%
$9,876 to $40,125	12%
$40,125 to $85,525	22%
 A. 
The tax payable is 22% of the taxable income.
 B. 
The average tax rate on the taxable income is 10%.
 C. 
The actual tax is more than 22% of the taxable income.
 D. 
A part of the taxable income is taxed at 12%.

D. A part of the taxable income is taxed at 12%. 

Explanation: 
- The first tax bracket ranges from $0 to $9,875 and is taxed at 10%
- The second tax bracket ranges from $9,876 to $40,125 and is taxed at 12%
- The third tax bracket ranges from $40,125 to $85,525 and is taxed at 22%

Kim's taxable income falls partially into both the second and third tax brackets. The amount in the second tax bracket ($40,125 - $9,876 = $30,249) is taxed at 12%, while the amount in the third tax bracket ($72,000 - $40,125 = $31,875) is taxed at 22%. Therefore, the actual tax payable is a combination of the tax paid on the income in both brackets.

Kim's taxable income falls partially into both the second and third tax brackets. Zander is searching for ways to decrease the amount of income taxes he pays each year. Which of the following ways can he accomplish this goal?

 A. 
Decrease the amount of tax credits
 B. 
Increase his income
 C. 
Decrease his expenses
 D. 
Increase the amount of itemized deductions
D. Increase the amount of itemized deductions.

Match each tax with its description.

employment
taxes
capital gains tax
income taxes
gift tax
payable by corporations
arrowRight
payable on profits from the sale
of stocks
arrowRight
payable on large amounts transferred
without receiving a significant return
arrowRight
payable on interest, dividends
and tips

employment taxes --> payable by corporations

capital gains tax --> payable on profits from the sale of stocks

gift tax --> payable on large amounts transferred without receiving a significant return

income taxes --> payable on interest, dividends and tips

Determine whether each type of tax is paid to a state or local government, to the federal government, or both.

sales tax
payroll tax
income tax
property tax

federal government . state or local government

sales tax --> state or local government

payroll tax --> both (employees pay both the Social Security and Medicare portions, while employers pay a matching amount)

income tax --> both (federal government collects federal income tax, while state governments may also collect state income tax)

property tax --> state or local government

Some taxcan be used more than once.
Determine whether each type of tax is paid to a state or local government, to the federal government, or both.

sales tax
payroll tax
income tax
property tax

federal government , state or local government

sales tax --> state or local government

payroll tax --> both (employees pay both the Social Security and Medicare portions, while employers pay a matching amount)

income tax --> both (federal government collects federal income tax, while state governments may also collect state income tax)

property tax --> state or local government

Tommy's grandparents recently gave him a check of $25,000 to support him through his years at college. Which tax might Tommy or his grandparents have to pay?

 A. 
Tommy: Inheritance tax; grandparents: none
 B. 
Tommy: gift tax; grandparents: none
 C. 
Tommy: none; grandparents: inheritance tax
 D. 
Tommy: none; grandparents: gift tax
D. Tommy: none; grandparents: gift tax.

A historian is writing a paper on the idea of the divine right of kings in Europe during the Middle Ages. She reads a speech by a bishop in the Middle Ages claiming that kings were appointed by God to rule over people. She then explains why the bishop would have believed this idea, even if it is not one that most people believe today. What is she doing in this paper?
 A. 
The historian is explaining cause and effect.
 B. 
The historian is placing the argument in historical context.
 C. 
The historian is comparing and contrasting primary sources.
 D. 
The historian is developing a hypothesis.
B. The historian is placing the argument in historical context.

Determine whether the following events concern Hernán Cortés or Francisco Pizarro.
execution of the last
Inca emperor, Atahualpa
spread of smallpox from
soldiers to the Aztec
capture of the territory
around Veracruz

execution of the last Inca emperor, Atahualpa --> Francisco Pizarro

spread of smallpox from soldiers to the Aztec --> Hernán Cortés

capture of the territory around Veracruz --> Hernán Cortés
